title: Maurice D. Hinchey Hudson River Valley National Heritage Area Enhancement Act
short_title: Maurice D. Hinchey Hudson River Valley National Heritage Area Enhancement Act
plain_summary: This bill asks the Secretary of the Interior to study whether two counties in New York should be added to a special area called the Hudson River Valley National Heritage Area. The two counties are Saratoga County and Washington County. The study will work with local history groups and tourism offices to see if adding these counties is a good idea and if it can be done. The goal is to figure out if these counties should become part of this protected heritage area along the Hudson River.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Maurice D. Hinchey Hudson River Valley National Heritage Area Enhancement Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Hudson River Valley NHA Study
sections.text:
This section explains what certain words mean in this law. The Secretary means the Secretary of the Interior, who is the person in charge of the federal department that manages national parks and heritage areas. The State means the State of New York. The study area means Saratoga County and Washington County in New York.

The Secretary must work together with people who know about preserving historic places, historical societies, tourism offices, and other appropriate groups and government agencies. The Secretary will conduct a study to see if it makes sense and if it is possible to include Saratoga County and Washington County as part of the Maurice D. Hinchey Hudson River Valley National Heritage Area. This heritage area was created by a law passed in nineteen ninety six called the Hudson River Valley National Heritage Area Act. The study must follow the rules written in section one hundred twenty thousand one hundred and three of title fifty four of the United States Code.
